title: Republicans Set to Block Feinstein Replacement Vote on Key Senate Committee
description: GOP opposition threatens Democrats' plan to replace ailing Sen. Feinstein with Ben Cardin on the powerful Judiciary Committee.
lede: Senate Republicans are preparing to block a Democratic effort to temporarily replace Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee. The 89-year-old California Democrat's extended absence has stalled key judicial nominations and sparked calls for her replacement.

sections.heading: Schumer's Replacement Plan
paragraphs: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer announced Tuesday his intention to replace Feinstein with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland on the Judiciary Committee. The move comes as Democrats struggle to advance judicial nominees without Feinstein's crucial vote on the evenly split 11-11 committee.

Schumer aims to hold a floor vote this afternoon, but Republicans have signaled they will block the effort. The procedural battle highlights the political stakes surrounding Feinstein's absence and the broader confirmation process for federal judges.

sections.heading: Feinstein's Extended Absence
paragraphs: Feinstein has been absent from the Senate since February due to health issues, including a bout with shingles that led to complications. Her office has provided limited updates on her condition, fueling speculation about her ability to return to full Senate duties.

The veteran senator's absence has effectively paralyzed the Judiciary Committee's ability to advance President Biden's judicial nominees. Without her vote, Democrats cannot move nominees out of committee, creating a significant bottleneck in the confirmation process.

sections.heading: GOP Opposition Strategy
paragraphs: Republicans argue that temporarily replacing a sitting senator on committees sets a dangerous precedent. They contend that Feinstein should either return to work or resign, rather than Democrats attempting to work around her absence through procedural maneuvers.

GOP leaders have indicated they view the replacement effort as an overreach that undermines Senate traditions. They're prepared to use procedural tools to prevent the vote from succeeding, despite Democratic control of the chamber.

sections.heading: Cardin as Replacement
paragraphs: Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland emerged as the Democratic choice to temporarily fill Feinstein's Judiciary Committee role. The longtime senator brings extensive legislative experience and has previously served on high-profile committees.

Cardin's selection reflects Democrats' need for a reliable vote who can immediately contribute to committee proceedings. His background in civil rights and constitutional law makes him a natural fit for the Judiciary Committee's work on federal court nominations.

sections.heading: Political Implications
paragraphs: The standoff over Feinstein's replacement underscores broader tensions about Senate operations and the confirmation process. Democrats face pressure to maintain momentum on Biden's judicial nominees, while Republicans see an opportunity to slow Democratic priorities.

The situation also highlights questions about aging lawmakers and their ability to fulfill Senate duties. At 89, Feinstein represents broader concerns about the advanced age of many senators and the impact on legislative effectiveness.

sections.heading: Path Forward
paragraphs: With Republicans expected to block today's vote, Democrats may need to explore alternative strategies for advancing judicial nominees. Options could include procedural maneuvers or renewed pressure for Feinstein to step down from the committee voluntarily.

The impasse threatens to further delay confirmation of federal judges at a time when courts face significant vacancies. The resolution of this standoff will likely shape the remainder of Biden's ability to reshape the federal judiciary.

faqs.q: Why can't Democrats just proceed without Feinstein?
faqs.a: The Judiciary Committee is evenly split 11-11 between Democrats and Republicans. Without Feinstein's vote, Democrats cannot advance nominees out of committee to the full Senate floor.

faqs.q: How long has Senator Feinstein been absent?
faqs.a: Feinstein has been absent from the Senate since February 2023 due to health complications from shingles, creating a months-long disruption to committee work.

faqs.q: What precedent would replacing Feinstein set?
faqs.a: Republicans argue that temporarily replacing a sitting senator on committees without their consent would establish a dangerous precedent that could be abused in future political situations.

faqs.q: Could Feinstein resign from just the committee?
faqs.a: Yes, senators can resign from specific committees while remaining in office. This would allow Democrats to appoint a replacement without the current political battle.

faqs.q: How many judicial nominees are affected?
faqs.a: Dozens of federal judge nominees are awaiting committee action, with the backlog growing during Feinstein's extended absence from Senate duties.
